actor: NIST Cybersecurity Center of Excellence (NCCoE) project lead drafting the non-human-identity control profile
job: scope and publish a dedicated non-human-identity control profile (SP 800-207 Zero Trust supplement or SP 800-53 control-family overlay) that names machine identities, agent identities, and service-account identities a distinct compliance category with discovery, lifecycle, least-privilege, and audit-trail control families.
outcome: Public draft (initial public draft + final) of the NHI control profile with mapped controls, reference architectures, and a community-of-interest comment cycle closed before federal CISO adoption windows.
motivation: Cisco's $400M Astrix M&A plus Microsoft Agent 365 GA signal the category has crossed into balance-sheet materiality; without a published-standard reference, federal and banking buyers cannot procure against vendor-specific NHI architectures.

task.who: NIST Computer Security Division + NCCoE project team + community-of-interest contributors from Cisco, Microsoft, Okta, Saviynt, SailPoint, and federal agency reviewers
task.what: control-profile document with mapped SP 800-53 controls, reference architectures for agent-identity discovery and lifecycle, and a cross-walk to Zero Trust Architecture pillars
task.where: NCCoE project site + nccoe.nist.gov public draft + federal register comment dockets
task.when: scoping March-May 2026, initial public draft Q3 2026, final by Q4 2026
task.why: the ~7,000 internet-exposed MCP servers (about half unauthenticated) and 100:1 NHI-to-human ratio leave the federal/banking buyer tier without a procurement-grade standard
task.how: convene a community-of-interest workshop, harvest existing vendor architectures, map controls to SP 800-53 Rev 5 + SP 800-207, run the standard NIST public-comment cycle

actor: Saviynt VP of product management for the converged identity governance platform
job: align the Saviynt agent-identity and machine-identity governance roadmap to the emerging NIST control profile so the H2 2026 release ships against the published-standard control families rather than a vendor-defined NHI taxonomy.
outcome: Saviynt platform release with a published NIST-NHI-profile control-mapping matrix, audit-trail attestation reports, and a discovery module covering MCP-server endpoints and service-account inventories.
motivation: First IGA vendor to ship against the NIST profile wins federal-CIO and Tier-1-bank RFPs against Cisco-Astrix and Microsoft Entra-Agent-ID before the de-facto two-vendor control plane locks in.

task.who: Saviynt product management + identity-engineering leads + federal-compliance team + outside auditor for control-mapping attestation
task.what: control-mapping matrix, MCP-endpoint discovery module, agent-lifecycle policy engine, and audit-trail attestation reports tied to the NIST profile control IDs
task.where: Saviynt converged platform + customer Identity Cloud tenants + federal authorization boundaries (FedRAMP High)
task.when: alignment to NIST initial public draft Q3 2026, GA aligned to final profile Q4 2026 to Q1 2027
task.why: the federal CISO buyer tier requires a published-standard reference and Cisco + Microsoft already own the two-vendor de-facto plane
task.how: track NIST community-of-interest workshops, instrument the discovery + lifecycle stack to emit control-IDs, ship the mapping matrix as a customer-facing compliance artifact

actor: Fortune-500 financial-services CISO commenting on the NIST initial public draft
job: submit substantive public-comment feedback that ties the proposed NHI control families back to concrete operational findings — MCP-server inventory gaps, service-account proliferation, and agentic-AI lateral-movement scenarios already observed inside the bank's own Microsoft Agent 365 and Anthropic-finance-agent deployments.
outcome: Filed public comment (and an industry-coalition joint comment with FS-ISAC peers) that shapes the final control profile to include audit-trail attribution requirements compatible with SR 11-7 model-risk and SEC cyber-disclosure reporting.
motivation: Avoid being forced into a control profile that mismatches existing model-risk and cyber-disclosure controls — better to invest comment cycles upfront than retrofit a published standard against bank-specific operational reality.

task.who: bank CISO office + identity governance lead + chief privacy officer + FS-ISAC Identity Working Group + outside counsel reviewing the comment letter
task.what: written public-comment letter with annotated control-by-control feedback, an operational-evidence appendix, and a joint FS-ISAC coalition signature block
task.where: NIST federal register docket + FS-ISAC member channels + the bank's GRC Committee record
task.when: comment window Q3 2026 (typically 60-90 days from initial public draft posting)
task.why: agentic-AI inside Tier-1 banks already chains tools and MCP data brokers across core systems in minutes; a published profile that ignores this operational reality forces costly retrofitting
task.how: internal red-team review of the bank's NHI inventory, FS-ISAC working-group consensus call, formal letter via the bank's federal-affairs office
